headline: Apple's new CEO is a product perfectionist taking on the AI age
summary: To understand how Apple's new CEO John Ternus will run the company, pay attention to what he refuses to sell.  "We never think about shipping a technology," Ternus, 50, said in a recent interview about AI ‌with tech review site Tom's Guide.  "We always think about how can we leverage technology to ship amazing products."

content.title: Apple's new CEO is a product perfectionist taking on the AI age
content.summary: To understand how Apple's new CEO John Ternus will run the company, pay attention to what he refuses to sell.  "We never think about shipping a technology," Ternus, 50, said in a recent interview about AI ‌with tech review site Tom's Guide.  "We always think about how can we leverage technology to ship amazing products."

headline: Australia's Syenta raises $26 million to ease AI chip bottleneck, former Intel CEO joins board
summary: Syenta, an Australian semiconductor technology startup, said on Tuesday that it has raised $26 million for a new manufacturing technique ‌that could help ease persistent supply chain bottlenecks for artificial intelligence.  Along with the ‌funding, the company said it will open an office in the U.S. state of Arizona, near the manufacturing facilities ​of Intel and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co, and that former Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger will be joining its board of directors.  Modern AI chips such as those from Nvidia, Google and others are not a single chip, but rather multiple chips bonded together with what is known as ‌advanced packaging technology, mostly from TSMC.

content.title: Australia's Syenta raises $26 million to ease AI chip bottleneck, former Intel CEO joins board
content.summary: Syenta, an Australian semiconductor technology startup, said on Tuesday that it has raised $26 million for a new manufacturing technique ‌that could help ease persistent supply chain bottlenecks for artificial intelligence.  Along with the ‌funding, the company said it will open an office in the U.S. state of Arizona, near the manufacturing facilities ​of Intel and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co, and that former Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger will be joining its board of directors.  Modern AI chips such as those from Nvidia, Google and others are not a single chip, but rather multiple chips bonded together with what is known as ‌advanced packaging technology, mostly from TSMC.

headline: Apple vs. Microsoft: Which is better positioned based on leadership?
summary: Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook announced that he will be stepping down effective September 1, transitioning from chief executive to executive chairman. Between Apple and Microsoft (MSFT) — which continues to be led by chairman and CEO Satya Nadella — which tech stock is currently better positioned? Visible Alpha head of TMT research Melissa Otto, B. Riley Wealth chief market strategist Art Hogan, and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma compare and contrast the two companies amid the latest AI trends.

Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook announced that he will be stepping down effective September 1, transitioning from chief executive to executive chairman.

Between Apple and Microsoft (MSFT) — which continues to be led by chairman and CEO Satya Nadella — which tech stock is currently better positioned?

Visible Alpha head of TMT research Melissa Otto, B. Riley Wealth chief market strategist Art Hogan, and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma compare and contrast the two companies amid the latest AI trends.

headline: Newsweek Names Integrity One of America's Greatest Workplaces for Culture, Belonging & Community
summary: Integrity, LLC ("Integrity"), a leading distributor of life and health insurance, and provider of wealth management and retirement planning solutions, today announced it has been recognized by Newsweek as one of America's Greatest Workplaces for Culture, Belonging & Community. This prestigious award honors the company's emphasis on creating an environment where employees feel valued, heard and connected. Other winners include American Express, Google, GE, Starbucks, 3M, Accenture and Adobe.

headline: Heard on the Street | Apple’s New Boss Needs to Think a Little More Different
summary: Apple surprised no one by naming John Ternus as its new chief executive.  Apple spent much of Tim Cook’s tenure as CEO in the number-one slot.  Apple may not be spending hundreds of billions of dollars to build up supercharged AI networks, but its lack of any distinctive foothold in the disruptive technology has made the company look like a laggard.

Apple may not be spending hundreds of billions of dollars to build up supercharged AI networks, but its lack of any distinctive foothold in the disruptive technology has made the company look like a laggard.

headline: TD Visa Credit Card Customers will be the first in Canada to use Virtual Card Numbers through Google as a payment option on Google Chrome

Investment research firm Morningstar is in advanced talks to lease roughly 300,000 square feet on the upper floors of the Thompson Center at 100 West Randolph Street, where the tech giant is currently executing a more than $300 million gut rehabilitation. headline: Microsoft must face $2.8 billion UK lawsuit over cloud computing licences
summary: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software ‌on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, ‌a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday.  Competition lawyer Maria Luisa Stasi is bringing the case on behalf ​of nearly 60,000 businesses that run Windows Server on rival cloud platforms.  They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because ‌Microsoft charges higher wholesale ⁠prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ⁠than Amazon's AWS or Google Cloud.

They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because ‌Microsoft charges higher wholesale ⁠prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ⁠than Amazon's AWS or Google Cloud.

headline: Intel results to show if supply chain issues are dimming its AI ambitions
summary: Intel investors on Thursday will focus on its efforts to sort out ‌supply chain issues that have limited the company's ‌ability to ramp up chip production to meet rising demand from businesses ​adopting AI-related services.  Intel has warned that supply constraints for its server chips that are used alongside graphics processors made by companies such as Nvidia will be most acute in ‌the first quarter before ⁠easing in the second.  • Intel is expected to report a 1.9% decline in first-quarter revenue ⁠to $12.42 billion and a near 90% drop in adjusted earnings per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

headline: Tredence Awarded 2026 Google Cloud Global Industry Solutions Partner of the Year Award for Retail
summary: Tredence, a global AI and data science solutions company, today announced it has been named the 2026 Google Cloud Services & Industry Solutions Partner of the Year for Retail. This award reflects Tredence's demonstrated ability to deliver measurable customer impact at scale. Notably, this is Tredence's third such distinction, having previously been recognized in CPG and Gaming, a testament to Tredence's unmatched depth, breadth, and impact across the industries it serves.

headline: Alphabet’s SpaceX Stake Puts Fresh Focus On Non Core Value

The stake ties Alphabet to SpaceX’s Starlink business and creates touchpoints with Google Cloud.
